narrative.description: Lumines (2005) is a acclaimed puzzle title recognized in retro gaming lists for its distinctive mechanics, pacing, and lasting influence on the genre.
narrative.core_loop: Place or match pieces under pressure to clear objectives and chase high scores.

mechanics.map_notes: Core grid placement puzzle loop defines Lumines's identity.
mechanics.expression: Player positions discrete shapes within a fixed grid, optimizing placement to achieve row or pattern completion goals.

mechanics.map_notes: Core fill reward progression loop loop defines Lumines's identity.
mechanics.expression: A progression gate where a fixed collection target must be met before an upgrade or skill point becomes available, tying advancement directly to measurable progress.

mechanics.map_notes: Core line clear scoring loop defines Lumines's identity.
mechanics.expression: Completing full horizontal rows in a grid clears them and awards points, often with bonuses for multiple simultaneous clears.

mechanics.map_notes: Lock Delay Placement supports the main loop in Lumines.
mechanics.expression: After a piece lands, a brief grace period allows final horizontal adjustments before it locks permanently.

mechanics.map_notes: Next Piece Preview supports the main loop in Lumines.
mechanics.expression: A display shows the upcoming piece or pieces, enabling advance planning of placement strategy.

mechanics.map_notes: Soft Drop Control supports the main loop in Lumines.
mechanics.expression: Holding a direction accelerates piece descent without instant lock, allowing faster placement at a score or risk tradeoff.

mechanics.map_notes: Hard Drop Placement supports the main loop in Lumines.
mechanics.expression: An instant-drop input locks the current piece at its horizontal position immediately.

mechanics.map_notes: Tetromino Rotation supports the main loop in Lumines.
mechanics.expression: Falling polyomino pieces rotate in fixed increments to fit into the playfield grid.

mechanics.map_notes: Multi Line Scoring Multiplier supports the main loop in Lumines.
mechanics.expression: Clearing multiple lines simultaneously awards disproportionately higher scores than single-line clears.

mechanics.map_notes: Life Based Retry System supports the main loop in Lumines.
mechanics.expression: A failure-buffer system in which the player is granted a limited number of retries, losing one life upon failure and restarting from a previous checkpoint.

mechanics.map_notes: Optional Content Layering supports the main loop in Lumines.
mechanics.expression: Hidden rooms, passages, or content zones exist off main paths, requiring players to investigate suspicious environments.

mechanics.map_notes: Mid Level Checkpoint supports the main loop in Lumines.
mechanics.expression: A save point within a stage resets the player to that position on death rather than restarting the entire level.

mechanics.map_notes: Escalating Drop Speed supports the main loop in Lumines.
mechanics.expression: The rate at which pieces fall increases over time or level, compressing decision windows as the session progresses.

mechanics.map_notes: Depletable Health Pool supports the main loop in Lumines.
mechanics.expression: A survival mechanic where the player character possesses a finite quantity of health points that decrease when taking damage, with depletion resulting in failure.

mechanics.map_notes: Telegraphed Threat Loop supports the main loop in Lumines.
mechanics.expression: A combat encounter structure in which an enemy signals an incoming attack through a distinct visual or audio cue before the attack resolves.

design_intent.player_fantasy: A puzzle experience centered on Lumines.
